definition: An instrument for writing or drawing with ink
example: One child, one teacher, one book, one pen can change the world.
speech: Address to the United Nations Youth Assembly

definition: A projectile fired from a gun
example: They thought that the bullets would silence us, but they failed.

definition: The absence of sound or noise
example: They thought that the bullets would silence us, but they failed. And out of that silence came thousands of voices.

definition: A strong desire to do or achieve something
example: The terrorists thought that they would change my aims and stop my ambitions. But nothing changed in my life except this: weakness, fear and hopelessness died. Strength, power and courage were born.

definition: The ability to do something that frightens one; bravery
example: The terrorists thought that they would change my aims and stop my ambitions. But nothing changed in my life except this: weakness, fear and hopelessness died. Strength, power and courage were born.

definition: The sound produced in a person's larynx and uttered through the mouth
example: I speak not for myself, but so those without a voice can be heard. Those who have fought for their rights. Their right to live in peace. Their right to be treated with dignity. Their right to equality of opportunity. Their right to be educated.

definition: The state of being equal, especially in status, rights, and opportunities
example: Their right to live in peace. Their right to be treated with dignity. Their right to equality of opportunity. Their right to be educated.

definition: The state or quality of being worthy of honor or respect
example: Their right to live in peace. Their right to be treated with dignity.

definition: A person who uses unlawful violence and intimidation, especially against civilians, in the pursuit of political aims
example: The terrorists thought that they would change my aims and stop my ambitions.

definition: A person who holds extreme political or religious views
example: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.

definition: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction
example: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.

definition: The unlawful use of violence and intimidation, especially against civilians, in the pursuit of political aims
example: With guns you can kill terrorists, with education you can kill terrorism.
speech: Multiple speeches on education vs. violence

definition: Intense or passionate dislike
example: I do not even hate the Talib who shot me. Even if there is a gun in my hand and he stands in front of me, I would not shoot him.

definition: A weapon incorporating a metal tube from which bullets, shells, or other missiles are propelled by explosive force
example: I do not even hate the Talib who shot me. Even if there is a gun in my hand and he stands in front of me, I would not shoot him.

definition: The ability or capacity to do something or act in a particular way
example: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.

definition: A written or printed work consisting of pages glued or sewn together along one side and bound in covers
example: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.

definition: An instrument for writing or drawing with ink, typically consisting of a metal nib or ball, or a nylon tip, fitted into a metal or plastic holder
example: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.

definition: A woman or girl in relation to other daughters and sons of her parents
example: We call upon our sisters around the world to be brave — to embrace the strength within themselves and realize their full potential.

definition: Ready to face and endure danger or pain; showing courage
example: We call upon our sisters around the world to be brave — to embrace the strength within themselves and realize their full potential.

definition: Having or showing the capacity to develop into something in the future
example: We call upon our sisters around the world to be brave — to embrace the strength within themselves and realize their full potential.
